input.headline: Korean coast guard arrests helmsman who was on his phone when ferry ran aground

raw.headline: Korean coast guard arrests helmsman who was on his phone when ferry ran aground

winner: The helmsman smirked as he showed off his brand-new "I Survived the Ferry" keychain—then realized too late that the one he really needed was for his phone’s lock screen.

candidates.text: Why did the ferry captain get arrested for texting? Because he thought he was playing "Tipsy Boat"!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the pun on 'Tipsy Boat,' but it's a bit forced and lacks the specificity of the original headline. It also doesn't fully tie into the context of being arrested for using a phone while operating a ferry."}

candidates.text: I told my GPS we're taking a scenic route, but I think it meant the one that leads straight into a coast guard's custody for texting and driving.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat relevant and has a mild twist, but it lacks the specificity and surprise that would make it stand out in a humor competition."}

candidates.text: The helmsman smirked as he showed off his brand-new "I Survived the Ferry" keychain—then realized too late that the one he really needed was for his phone’s lock screen.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat funny but lacks the sharp punchline needed for maximum impact. It's specific to the situation and uses natural English, but the humor feels a bit forced and less surprising compared to what could be achieved with more creative wordplay."}

candidates.text: Apparently, the captain's excuse that 'I was just checking my texts' is now a new defense in maritime law—turns out, 'swipe right for disaster' isn't just bad dating advice.
The captain of the grounded ferry said the accident was avoidable—he just didn’t see any water in front of him.
